jokes.text: In a world of 31 flavors, we're the cup of water they rinse the scoops in.
jokes.setup: Grampa lamenting the Simpson family's boring heritage
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to the most mundane part of an ice cream shop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor with perfect economy - captures the Simpson family's ordinariness with an unexpected but completely apt comparison

jokes.text: Grampa out.
jokes.setup: Dramatic exit line after delivering family insult
jokes.punchline: Casual mic-drop style departure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa character beat - abrupt, dramatic, and perfectly timed

jokes.text: We should take lots of pictures of him while we still can. Camera's broken!
jokes.setup: Marge's sweet suggestion about documenting Grampa
jokes.punchline: Immediate practical obstacle ruins the moment
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom beat - sentimental moment immediately undercut by mundane reality

jokes.text: With some clever scissor work, I can make the Lake Land Butter Indian maiden show me her boobs.
jokes.setup: Bart examining butter packaging
jokes.punchline: Juvenile manipulation of innocent imagery
jokes.explanation: Very Bart character moment - combines his mischief with inappropriate humor typical of his age

jokes.text: Clean, dirty.
jokes.setup: Bart demonstrating his butter package trick
jokes.punchline: Simple binary description of the visual transformation
jokes.explanation: Economical visual gag payoff - Bart's matter-of-fact presentation adds to the humor

jokes.text: Look what Snap's doing to Pop while Crackle watches!
jokes.setup: Bart examining cereal box characters
jokes.punchline: Implying inappropriate activity between cartoon mascots
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of innocent cartoon imagery - Bart finding innuendo everywhere is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hitachee tribe
jokes.setup: Lisa creating fake Native American heritage
jokes.punchline: Tribe name obviously derived from 'Hitachi' brand
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sets up the entire episode's central joke - Lisa's fake heritage based on appliance brand

jokes.text: Lisa, the world needs you to go to Wellesley!
jokes.setup: Discussion about school project grades
jokes.punchline: Marge's overwrought concern about prestigious college admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge parenting anxiety - turning elementary school project into life-or-death college admission scenario

jokes.text: I didn't come here to be insulted. Guten tag!
jokes.setup: Lisa's presentation about European destruction of Native Americans
jokes.punchline: German person taking personal offense and leaving
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - historical guilt made personal and immediate

jokes.text: By speaking with forked tongue, I am in heap big trouble. And now I'm thinking in stereotypes! That's even worse!
jokes.setup: Lisa realizing she's lying about her heritage
jokes.punchline: Self-aware recognition of her own stereotypical thinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary - Lisa catches herself perpetuating stereotypes while lying about heritage, very character-appropriate self-awareness

jokes.text: Must be the war cry of her tribe. Probably.
jokes.setup: Lisa screaming in frustration
jokes.punchline: Parents' casual misinterpretation as tribal behavior
jokes.explanation: Good parental obliviousness beat - they completely miss Lisa's distress

jokes.text: Still?! Are you drownin' 'em or makin' love to 'em?
jokes.setup: Brandine still drowning rats in the tub
jokes.punchline: Cletus's disturbing alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Cletus inappropriateness - combining hillbilly stereotypes with disturbing implications

jokes.text: Why? You're not helping me. But, sir, I'm flaming!
jokes.setup: Willie on fire asking Burns for help
jokes.punchline: Burns's cold logical response to emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Burns callousness - treating a burning man as a business transaction

jokes.text: These are drier than a Charles Grodin quip.
jokes.setup: Willie trying to use wet wipes to fight fire
jokes.punchline: Comparing dryness to obscure celebrity's humor style
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific cultural reference - Willie's colorful metaphor using obscure celebrity comparison

jokes.text: My last words are 'Lisa sucks!'
jokes.setup: Bart thinking he's about to die in fire
jokes.punchline: Using final moments to insult his sister
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's priorities - even facing death, sibling rivalry takes precedence over profundity

jokes.text: No way! I don't want my guts touching some sick guy.
jokes.setup: Being asked about organ donation on driver's license
jokes.punchline: Bart's juvenile understanding of organ transplants
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - Bart's misunderstanding of how organ donation works is age-appropriate

jokes.text: Oh, this is a bad time for me to have massive organ failure
jokes.setup: Overhearing Bart refuse to be organ donor
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate timing of medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good timing gag - coincidental medical emergency right after organ donation refusal

jokes.text: Eat Marshall Tucker, idiot!
jokes.setup: Homer road raging at another driver
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical insult referencing obscure band
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random Homer insult - combining road rage with completely irrelevant 70s rock band reference

jokes.text: They had seven names for that.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing awkward sidewalk dance phenomenon
jokes.punchline: Fake tribe having excessive terminology for minor social interaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lisa's fabrication - parodying the 'Eskimos have 50 words for snow' trope

jokes.text: My little girl's just like CBS News.
jokes.setup: Lisa admitting her report wasn't fact-checked
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing her to famously discredited news organization
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that works on multiple levels - Homer's pride in Lisa's 'journalism' is perfectly misplaced

jokes.text: North Dakota-lahoma.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about tribe's location
jokes.punchline: Lisa's panic-invented nonsense state name
jokes.explanation: Good panic humor - Lisa's transparent attempt to sound knowledgeable while making up geography

jokes.text: I kid, but he's a real jerk.
jokes.setup: Mentioning boss named 'Screaming Editor'
jokes.punchline: Clarifying the obvious stereotype is actually accurate
jokes.explanation: Minor beat - subverts expectation that stereotypical name is a joke

jokes.text: Hey, hey, LBJ, how many pants you rip today?
jokes.setup: 1960s themed Hulk toy
jokes.punchline: Anti-war chant adapted for Hulk's clothing destruction
jokes.explanation: Clever mashup of Vietnam War protest chant with Hulk mythology. Good cultural reference humor.

jokes.text: Yeah. I have that disease that makes you look like an old man, but they gave me medicine for it and I took too much.
jokes.setup: Girl commenting that Bart looks young
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated reverse aging disease explanation
jokes.explanation: Creative lie from Bart - absurd but delivered with confidence, typical of his quick thinking

jokes.text: They're gonna cut my head off...
jokes.setup: Homer calling about ketchup vs catsup emergency
jokes.punchline: Life-or-death stakes for trivial condiment question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Homer turning minor question into life-threatening situation

jokes.text: That is such a girl thing to say.
jokes.setup: Darcy expressing romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive 10-year-old response
jokes.explanation: Perfect age-appropriate response - Bart's immaturity showing through romantic situation

jokes.text: Oh, man, I must be the first guy who was ever pressured into marriage.
jokes.setup: Darcy pressuring Bart to marry her
jokes.punchline: Bart's oblivious comment about universal male experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Bart thinks he's unique in experiencing something virtually all men experience

jokes.text: Really? We didn't? But we kissed and held hands at the same time. Wow, you really are ten. I thought you were just kind of stupid.
jokes.setup: Confusion about pregnancy and paternity
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent confusion revealing his true age
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bart's genuine innocence about reproduction reveals his actual age

jokes.text: A Norwegian exchange student. He's long gone, back to Norwegia.
jokes.setup: Explaining who the real father is
jokes.punchline: Made-up country name 'Norwegia' instead of Norway
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism joke. Shows character's lack of education but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm ten and stupid.
jokes.setup: Darcy's assessment of Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character truth - Bart's self-aware admission is both funny and endearing

jokes.text: Home of America's most powerful weirdos?
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting Utah for marriage
jokes.punchline: Darcy's assessment of Utah's reputation
jokes.explanation: Standard Utah/Mormon joke. Functional humor but relies on common stereotypes.

jokes.text: I took the name from my microwave.
jokes.setup: Lisa confessing about fake tribe
jokes.punchline: Revealing the mundane source of 'Hitachee' name
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's central setup - reveals the obvious source we should have seen coming

jokes.text: I'm not Native American, either. I just ski a lot.
jokes.setup: Reporter who seemed authentically Native American
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just has a tan from skiing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - everyone's pretending to be Native American for various reasons

jokes.text: I'm Japanese. I'm an Aborigine. I'm two midgets in a costume.
jokes.setup: Mass confession of fake identities
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd revelations, ending with physical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity. The two midgets reveal is the perfect absurd cap to the sequence.

jokes.text: I'm two midgets in a costume.
jokes.setup: Series of fake Native American reveals
jokes.punchline: Most absurd revelation in the sequence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation topper - pushes the reveal sequence to absurd conclusion

jokes.text: Take my wife... please. Hey, I finally get that joke.
jokes.setup: Bart dealing with demanding pregnant girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Finally understanding classic Henny Youngman joke structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Bart's situation giving him context to understand classic comedy structure

jokes.text: I mean, what kind of tramp gets knocked up before she's even married? I did.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing unwed pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization she did the same thing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Marge's hypocrisy. The immediate self-awareness is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I mean in this day and age, when the link between sex and pregnancy has been proven so... conclusively... in Scientific American
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing teen pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Absurd citation of magazine as source for basic biological facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating basic reproduction as recent scientific discovery

jokes.text: Actually, that was me.
jokes.setup: Discussing $200 bubble gum charge
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting to childish purchase
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Homer's childish impulse purchases revealed

jokes.text: What are you, gay?
jokes.setup: Bart saying he only wants to marry one bride
jokes.punchline: Polygamy-normalizing Utah stereotype
jokes.explanation: Utah polygamy stereotype played for shock - works but relies on cultural assumption

jokes.text: Utah karate!
jokes.setup: Father attacking Bart
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical regional martial arts variation
jokes.explanation: Random regional fighting style - absurd but memorable battle cry

jokes.text: We can lie to the neighbors and tell them they're twins.
jokes.setup: Learning about both pregnancies
jokes.punchline: Suggesting deceptive explanation for timing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family shame and social deception. Moderately clever solution to embarrassment.

jokes.text: Is that Bart? And I'll go... and you'll know.
jokes.setup: Bart's farewell to Darcy
jokes.punchline: Promise of future recognition through burping
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Bart's romantic gesture is perfectly age-appropriate

jokes.text: Instead we just ended up fighting all the time. Yeah, how 'bout that?
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on his brief relationship experience
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual confirmation of marriage reality
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about marriage reality. Homer's weary acceptance is character-perfect.

jokes.text: And someday you'll be one, too.
jokes.setup: Homer saying Bart will be a great father
jokes.punchline: Bart's backhanded compliment about Homer's future parenting
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic - Bart's subtle dig at Homer's current parenting skills
